hypothesis: A seam lag growing with frontier width might yield an exact recurrence linking finite support to the opposite characteristic boundary.
interpretation: The high frontier of every ordinary zero-tail state is exactly a shifted Rule 30 evolution and every fixed frontier window is eventually dyadically periodic. This supplies a genuine growing-lag recurrence but does not couple it to the aperiodic low schedule across the right-permutive middle.
limitations: does not prove the alternating inverse lift has infinite support
does not exclude eventual center period two
does not solve Rule 30 center nonperiodicity
finite functional graphs are exhausted only through prefix width 16
the all-width dyadic recurrence is the proof-note induction, not an inference from finite cycle data
neither characteristic boundary alone bridges the growing middle
proof_scope: The reversal conjugacy, branch-independent safe high prefix, and dyadic temporal-period induction are all-width statements proved in proofs/informal/problem1_period_two_characteristic_front.md. Integer and finite-prefix graph campaigns are exact bounded regression checks.
question: problem1

exact_conclusions.characteristic_self_embedding: the schedule-independent high frontier of every ordinary zero-tail state is an exact shifted Rule 30 orbit
exact_conclusions.dyadic_front_recurrence: every fixed high-front window is eventually periodic with a power-of-two period
exact_conclusions.remaining_target: a cross-characteristic quantity spanning both boundaries
exact_conclusions.two_boundary_strip: a dyadically recurrent high boundary and aperiodic low fringe coexist across a growing right-permutive interior

prefix_periods.exact_inductive_bound: column j is eventually periodic with period dividing 2^(j+1); a width-r prefix therefore has dyadic eventual period dividing 2^r

zero_tail_front.boundary_layer: only the final four low-end positions can depend on q
